title: T-008 — shelf_life_rules table migration
prompt:
# T-008 — shelf_life_rules table migration

PRD: `05-WAREHOUSE-PRD.md` §5.1, §12.5.
Project root: `/Users/mariuszkrawczyk/Projects/monopilot-kira`.

## Goal
Create `shelf_life_rules` (customer/product min shelf life) — schema + API only in P1.

## Implementation contract
Add `packages/db/src/schema/shelf-life-rules.ts`: id, tenant_id, customer_id (nullable for global rules), product_id, min_shelf_life_days INT, enforced BOOLEAN, reason TEXT, created_at/by.
UNIQUE(tenant_id, customer_id, product_id) (with NULL customer_id treated as global; rely on partial unique idx where customer_id IS NULL).
CHECK min_shelf_life_days > 0.

## Files (scope)
- packages/db/src/schema/shelf-life-rules.ts [create]
- packages/db/migrations/0NN_shelf_life_rules.sql [create]
- packages/db/src/schema/shelf-life-rules.test.ts [create]

## Acceptance criteria
1. Given the migration runs, when shelf_life_rules is inspected, then PRD §12.5 columns are present.
2. Given two rows with same (tenant_id, customer_id, product_id), when the second commits, then DB raises a unique violation.
3. Given INSERT with min_shelf_life_days=0, when committed, then DB raises a CHECK violation.

## Test strategy
- RED: shelf-life-rules.test.ts asserts unique and CHECK violations.
- Run `pnpm --filter db migrate && pnpm --filter db test shelf-life-rules`.

## Risk red lines
- Do not implement pick-time enforcement here (P2 11-SHIPPING).
- Do not embed customer override logic.
- Do not embed service logic.

pipeline_inputs.description: Create shelf_life_rules table for customer/product minimum shelf-life enforcement (P1: schema + API only).
pipeline_inputs.details: Drizzle schema + migration. UNIQUE(tenant_id, customer_id, product_id); CHECK min_shelf_life_days > 0. Pick-time enforcement deferred to P2.
